title: Updating the status of all sub-items, based on the parent item's status
excerpt:
Hi, 
I would like to be able to update all of my sub-items to a completed status based on the completion of the parent item. I do not want to have to change the status of every sub-item individually. I have looked but th…

posts.author: mfysh (Matthew Fysh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

I would like to be able to update all of my sub-items to a completed status based on the completion of the parent item. I do not want to have to change the status of every sub-item individually. I have looked but there doesn’t seem to be a obvious solution to this.

Are you able to assist please?

Thanks

Matt

posts.author: chameleonseifu (Joy Sterling White)
posts.cookedHtml: @mfysh any luck with this? I have a need to automate assigning dates and statuses of a subitem based on the status of the parent item, but I haven’t seen that this is possible

posts.author: pbswilco (Shelly)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m in the same boat. It seems there are multiple variations of this question and I would hope a solution comes forward soon.

posts.author: Fantasy-Media-Team (Fantasy Media Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Shelly & Joy!

We developed an app that solves this exact pain point of subitems inherting column value from parent. It’s called Subitem Automations and it allows to do this on subitem creation and when the parent item changes, here’s an example -

On creation - Populate item’s data into its subitems in a seamless way

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.31.152226×400 30.1 KB

On change - Being able to set dependency between a subitem to an item

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.30.162106×432 27.6 KB

There are also more advanced options with offset of dates -

Screenshot 2023-09-11 at 18.36.032138×644 42.2 KB

It’s brand new (released last week) and we’re going to keep on adding new capabilities.

You can find it in the monday.com marketplace in the platform or here - https://monday.com/marketplace/10000287

Hope that helps!

posts.author: workflowboost (Bartosz @ Workflow Boost)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

You can use our Subitems Pro Automations to achieve that:

  • Copy parent status, date or any other column to all subitems

    image670×536 21.1 KB

    image668×534 22.1 KB

  • Changes all subitems status to specific value when item status changes to something

    image662×526 20.3 KB

If you have any questions or requests for custom automations, feel free to reach out to us!
